mutex.c(3) Library Functions Manual mutex.c(3)

NAME

mutex.c - Platform-agnostic mutex hooks and utilities.

SYNOPSIS

Functions


void mutex_on_lock (mutex_t *mutex)
Hook called when a mutex is successfully locked. void mutex_on_unlock (mutex_t *mutex)
Hook called when a mutex is unlocked. void mutex_on_trylock (mutex_t *mutex, bool success)
Hook called when a mutex trylock is attempted. int mutex_format_state (const mutex_t *mutex, char *buffer, size_t size)
Format mutex timing and state info into buffer. void mutex_log_state (const mutex_t *mutex, const char *file, int line, const char *func)
Log the state of a mutex.

Detailed Description

Platform-agnostic mutex hooks and utilities.

This file provides hook functions that can be called from platform-specific mutex implementations to record events and debug information. All hooks are debug-only and disabled in release builds.

Definition in file platform/mutex.c.

Function Documentation

int mutex_format_state (const mutex_t * mutex, char * buffer, size_t size)

Format mutex timing and state info into buffer.

Parameters

mutex Pointer to the mutex
buffer Output buffer
size Buffer size

Returns

Number of bytes written

Formats timing (lock/unlock), held-by info, and operation counts. Called by mutex_log_state() and --sync-state display code.

Definition at line 86 of file platform/mutex.c.

86                                                                         {
87   if (!mutex || !buffer || size == 0) return 0;
88 
89   int offset = 0;
90   uint64_t now_ns = time_get_ns();
91 
92   // Format timing information
93   char lock_str[64] = "";
94   char unlock_str[64] = "";
95   char held_str[64] = "";
96   char count_str[256] = "";
97 
98   if (mutex->last_lock_time_ns > 0 && mutex->last_lock_time_ns <= now_ns) {
99     char elapsed_str[64];
100     time_pretty(now_ns - mutex->last_lock_time_ns, -1, elapsed_str, sizeof(elapsed_str));
101     snprintf(lock_str, sizeof(lock_str), "lock=%s", elapsed_str);
102   }
103 
104   if (mutex->last_unlock_time_ns > 0 && mutex->last_unlock_time_ns <= now_ns) {
105     char elapsed_str[64];
106     time_pretty(now_ns - mutex->last_unlock_time_ns, -1, elapsed_str, sizeof(elapsed_str));
107     snprintf(unlock_str, sizeof(unlock_str), "unlock=%s", elapsed_str);
108   }
109 
110   if (mutex->currently_held_by_key != 0) {
111     snprintf(held_str, sizeof(held_str), "[LOCKED_BY=thread.%lu]", (unsigned long)mutex->currently_held_by_key);
112   }
113 
114   if (mutex->lock_count > 0 || mutex->unlock_count > 0 || mutex->trylock_count > 0) {
115     snprintf(count_str, sizeof(count_str), "[ops: lock=%lu unlock=%lu trylock=%lu/%lu]",
116              mutex->lock_count, mutex->unlock_count, mutex->trylock_success_count, mutex->trylock_count);
117   }
118 
119   offset += snprintf(buffer + offset, size - offset, "%s %s %s %s", lock_str, unlock_str, held_str, count_str);
120   return offset;
121 }




References mutex, time_get_ns(), and
    time_pretty().


Referenced by mutex_log_state().






void
  mutex_log_state (const mutex_t * mutex, const char * file, int line, const
  char * func)


Log the state of a mutex.


Parameters


mutex Pointer to the mutex


file Source file of the caller


line Source line of the caller


func Source function of the caller


Formats and logs detailed state information about the mutex.


Definition at line 134 of file platform/mutex.c.


134                                                                                          {
135   if (!mutex) return;
136   char buf[512];
137   mutex_format_state(mutex, buf, sizeof(buf));
138   log_msg(LOG_DEBUG, file, line, func, "mutex/state %p: %s", (const void *)mutex, buf);
139 }






References log_msg(), mutex, and
    mutex_format_state().






void
  mutex_on_lock (mutex_t * mutex)


Hook called when a mutex is successfully locked.


Parameters


mutex Pointer to the mutex that
  was locked


Called by platform-specific mutex_lock_impl() after the
    lock is acquired. Records timing, held-by thread, and increments lock
  count.


Definition at line 29 of file platform/mutex.c.


29                                    {
30   if (!mutex) return;
31   mutex->last_lock_time_ns = time_get_ns();
32   asciichat_thread_t current_thread = (asciichat_thread_t)asciichat_thread_current_id();
33   mutex->currently_held_by_key = asciichat_thread_to_key(current_thread);
34   mutex->lock_count++;
35 }






References asciichat_thread_current_id(), mutex, and
    time_get_ns().






void
  mutex_on_trylock (mutex_t * mutex, bool success)


Hook called when a mutex trylock is attempted.


Parameters


mutex Pointer to the mutex


success Whether the trylock succeeded


Called by platform-specific mutex_trylock_impl() after the
    attempt. Records timing and success/failure statistics.


Definition at line 63 of file platform/mutex.c.


63                                                     {
64   if (!mutex) return;
65   mutex->trylock_count++;
66   if (success) {
67     mutex->trylock_success_count++;
68     mutex->last_lock_time_ns = time_get_ns();
69     asciichat_thread_t current_thread = (asciichat_thread_t)asciichat_thread_current_id();
70     mutex->currently_held_by_key = asciichat_thread_to_key(current_thread);
71   }
72 }






References asciichat_thread_current_id(), mutex, and
    time_get_ns().






void
  mutex_on_unlock (mutex_t * mutex)


Hook called when a mutex is unlocked.


Parameters


mutex Pointer to the mutex that
  was unlocked


Called by platform-specific mutex_unlock_impl() before
    releasing the lock. Records timing and increments unlock count.


Definition at line 46 of file platform/mutex.c.


46                                      {
47   if (!mutex) return;
48   mutex->last_unlock_time_ns = time_get_ns();
49   mutex->currently_held_by_key = 0;
50   mutex->unlock_count++;
51 }






References mutex, and time_get_ns().
